stemHtml: Compared with day operations, what differences should a pilot adopt when seeking to identify a possible collision threat at night?

options.a: Spend twice as much time with the eyes remaining still during each saccade of the lookout scan.
options.b: Move the eyes in small arcs to induce a moving image on the retina for the rods to identify.
options.c: Focus on the instrument panel to allow the rods to identify objects outside.
options.d: Cover one eye to avoid being affected by the “blind spot”.
